es.exampleTranslation: Did you by any chance see my keys?
es.regionNote: Mexico also says 'de casualidad'. Reviewer to confirm which fronts.

it.example: Hai visto per caso le mie chiavi?
it.exampleTranslation: Have you by any chance seen my keys?

bridge.note: Same preposition for once (per/por) — the mismatch moved into the noun: Italian's clipped caso against Spanish's four-syllable casualidad. Each language thinks the other's version is the wrong length.

interference.es_to_it: 'Per casualità' overshoots — Italian keeps it short: per caso.
interference.it_to_es: 'Por caso' undershoots — Spanish spends the full noun: por casualidad (MX also 'de casualidad').

review.notes: From Joe's Italian teacher's prepositions crash course, 2026-07-16. ES pairing chosen by Claude, unverified — reviewer to confirm. Cue drafted by Claude 2026-07-20 from the entry's meaning and bridge — English situational framing, not a language claim, so it needs editorial taste rather than native review. Override freely.

cue: How do you ask whether by any chance?
